seoTitle: How to Stop and Turn Off Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Turn off Cloudflare for a domain using the zone pause feature, routing traffic straight to your origin server for troubleshooting or testing.
intro: There are times you'll want to temporarily stop Cloudflare from proxying your traffic, whether for troubleshooting a caching issue or testing your origin server directly. This guide covers pausing Cloudflare for a domain and confirming the change took effect.

steps.heading: Select your target domain
steps.body: From the Cloudflare dashboard, click into the specific website you want to stop protecting.

steps.heading: Scroll to Advanced Actions
steps.body: On the domain overview page, scroll down to find the Advanced Actions panel.

steps.heading: Click Pause Cloudflare on Site
steps.body: Select the pause link and confirm the security warning that appears.

steps.heading: Allow the propagation window
steps.body: Give the change roughly five minutes to take full effect across the edge network.

steps.heading: Confirm with a direct test
steps.body: Refresh the overview page to check the paused status, then run a terminal ping or dig test to confirm traffic is now hitting your origin server directly.

faq.question: Is pausing Cloudflare the same as removing the domain entirely?
faq.answer: No, pausing keeps your domain and DNS records set up in Cloudflare; it just stops the proxy from intercepting traffic until you reactivate it.

faq.question: Can I pause just one record instead of the whole domain?
faq.answer: Yes, switching an individual record to 'DNS Only' unproxies just that record, while the site-wide pause affects everything at once.

faq.question: How long does the pause take to fully apply?
faq.answer: Expect roughly a five-minute propagation window before the change is fully reflected across Cloudflare's network.

faq.question: Will my site lose its SSL certificate while paused?
faq.answer: Cloudflare's edge SSL handling is disabled during a pause, so your site depends on your origin server's own SSL certificate for the duration.
